title: What is MCP? Learn MCP Client, MCP Server & MCP Architecture
summary: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standard that enables AI applications to connect with external tools, APIs, databases, and services. The protocol defines client-server communication patterns using JSON-RPC and supports both local and remote servers via STDIO or HTTP streaming.
writeup: This video explains the Model Context Protocol (MCP), a standard for connecting AI applications to external tools, APIs, databases, and services. It breaks down the client-server architecture, including how MCP clients and servers communicate over JSON-RPC, and covers transport options for both local and remote servers via STDIO and HTTP streaming. The takeaway is a clear picture of MCP's communication patterns and how the protocol standardizes API and tool integration for AI agents.
